note: Health goals context. Primary objective: restore durable running capacity without reinjury. Running is primary psychological stabilizer and identity anchor.

next_race.name: Milwaukee Lakefront Half Marathon
next_race.urgency_note: 15 days out from today (2026-04-04). Hard calendar anchor. Coincides with Atlassian ShipIt hackathon window. Manage load accordingly — do not attempt a heroic training block in the final two weeks.

running.notes: Unable to run consistently for several months due to lower calf injury and related compensations. Chiropractic treatment ongoing since Nov 2025 focusing on hip balance, pelvic drift, rib positioning, and ankle mobility. Historical identity: multi-marathon runner with durable aerobic base (see Athlinks profile under Andy/Andrew Styx). Current objective is pain-free consistency, not pace recovery. Zone 2 effort acceptable regardless of pace regression.
running.psychological_note: Running is the primary emotional regulation mechanism and forward-motion signal. Missed runs accumulate as psychological debt faster than physical detraining. Minimum viable movement during high-load work periods matters more than optimal training.

active_program.name: 6-Week Periodized Lifting + Boxing + Running

structure.monday: Lower strength + Boxing (45 min lift, 20 min bag)
structure.wednesday: Upper strength + Boxing (45 min lift, 20 min bag)

phases.weeks_1_2: Strength Base — heavy compounds, moderate conditioning
phases.weeks_3_4: Hypertrophy + Conditioning — higher volume, increased boxing intensity
phases.weeks_5_6: Peaking — heavy singles/doubles, boxing at 85%+, deadlift PR attempt

active_program.rationale: Mirrors CrossFit 2012-2015 structure that drove sustained commitment: three distinct stimulus buckets (strength, skill/power, endurance), multiple progress vectors (bar numbers, technique, conditioning times), built-in variety prevents boredom.
active_program.calendar: Google Calendar events added for all 30 sessions (Apr 13 - May 24), 6:00 AM start

boxing.equipment: wavemaster (garage) + gym speed bag if available
boxing.notes: Boxing is the CrossFit-analogue separator — different enough from lifting to not cannibalize recovery, engaging enough that it doesn't feel like conditioning. Skill progression track alongside conditioning benefit. Integrated into Mon/Wed/Fri training days.

cardio_cross_training.notes: Peloton used to maintain aerobic continuity during running limitations. Substitute for missed runs without decision friction.

strength.notes: Prior 6 years CrossFit experience. Preference for compound lifts and simple repeatable templates. Emphasis on posterior chain, calf capacity, hip stability, core stiffness, shoulder alignment. New program: barbell compounds (squat, bench, deadlift, rows) as primary movements. Garage bar for heavy work, gym for accessories.

mobility_rehab.notes: Current constraints suggest kinetic chain imbalance rather than isolated muscle weakness. Daily minimum viable mobility maintains continuity.

sleep.notes: Sleep impacted by work cognitive load and stress accumulation. Maintain consistent sleep window rather than optimizing metrics.

nutrition.current_goal: gradual fat loss while maintaining running energy
default_patterns: protein-first ordering heuristic when eating out
nutrition.notes: Target weight trend toward 185-195 without aggressive restriction. Avoid extreme diet structure that increases cognitive load.

identity_context.primary_driver: running supports emotional regulation and sense of forward movement
identity_context.secondary_driver: strength training supports injury resilience and posture integrity
identity_context.risk_factor: stress and isolation degrade nutrition discipline and training consistency — high-load work periods are highest risk windows
identity_context.anchor: minimum weekly movement maintains psychological continuity even during high work load periods
identity_context.career_overlap: physical training consistency directly correlates with cognitive output quality — protecting health time is protecting work quality

ledger_alert_rules.note: LEDGER uses these to flag in weekly review.
